hypothesis: Training the multimodal contrastive model using isomeric (stereo-annotated) SMILES as symbolic anchors paired with multiple conformers per molecule will produce embeddings that remain SO(3)-invariant while becoming stereochemically discriminative, such that retrieval under unseen random rotations stays ≥75% Recall@10 and top-1 accuracy for distinguishing enantiomeric or diastereomeric pairs increases by ≥25% relative to a model trained with canonical (non-stereo) SMILES.
